title: IDF Airstrike Near Al-Qassam Mosque in Beit Lahia Kills Five Palestinians, Including Three Children
detail: An Israeli airstrike struck a group of civilians near the Al-Qassam mosque in Beit Lahia, northern Gaza, on April 23, 2026, killing five Palestinians — including three children. Bodies were transported to Al-Shifa Hospital in Gaza City. Gaza's Government Media Office cited the strike among more than 2,400 documented violations of the October 2025 ceasefire agreement, which include targeted strikes, arrests, blockades, and aid restrictions. Since the ceasefire began in October 2025, at least 786 Palestinians had been killed by Israeli forces before this strike — with 32 deaths recorded in April alone — bringing the post-ceasefire death toll to approximately 792 as of April 23. The cumulative Gaza war death toll since October 7, 2023 now stands at approximately 72,560+.

media.caption: Aftermath of Israeli airstrike near Al-Qassam mosque in Beit Lahia, killing five Palestinians including three children, April 23, 2026

title: Palestinian Man Awda Atef Awawdeh (25) Shot Dead by Israeli Settlers in Deir Dibwan — Third Settler Killing in Two Days
detail: Awda Atef Awawdeh, 25, was shot and killed by Israeli settlers in Deir Dibwan, a town east of Ramallah in the occupied West Bank, on April 23, 2026. His killing is the third fatal settler shooting in two days, following the deaths of two Palestinians at al-Mughayyir Boys School on April 21 and one Palestinian in Deir Dibwan on April 22. The April 21–23 wave constitutes the deadliest three-day settler violence episode of 2026 in the West Bank, with at least four Palestinians killed by settlers in 72 hours. Israeli security forces' response has consisted primarily of detentions of Palestinian residents rather than arrests of settlers — a pattern consistent with a 2025 Yesh Din report finding that 93.6% of settler violence investigations end without indictment.

media.caption: Continuing settler violence in the West Bank during Israel's Independence Day period — April 21–23, 2026

title: Israeli High Court Hears Bereaved Families' Petitions for Independent October 7 Commission of Inquiry
detail: Israel's High Court of Justice heard petitions on April 23, 2026 from bereaved families demanding an independent state commission of inquiry into the government's failures surrounding the October 7, 2023 Hamas attacks — two days after Israel's Memorial Day. The Israeli government argued that any investigation should be delayed until 'victory on all fronts,' and proposed a Knesset-controlled alternative rather than an independent commission. Former Prime Minister Naftali Bennett stated: 'Anyone who says later about investigating the October 7 massacre means never.' Multiple opposition parties — including Yesh Atid, Blue and White, Yisrael Beytenu, and the Democrats — pledged to establish an independent commission as their first act if elected. Bereaved families clashed outside the courthouse, reflecting deep divisions within Israeli society over accountability. The government's resistance mirrors its earlier resistance to commissions on settler violence, Lebanon, and the pre-Oct 7 intelligence failures.

media.caption: Bereaved families petition Israeli High Court for independent October 7 commission of inquiry, April 23, 2026

title: West Bank Protection Consortium Documents 16 Settler Sexual Violence Incidents Since Oct 2023; Cites Structural Impunity
detail: The West Bank Protection Consortium released findings on April 23, 2026 documenting at least 16 incidents of sexual violence — including sexual humiliation, rape threats, forced nudity, and urination on victims — committed by Israeli settlers and soldiers against Palestinians since October 2023. The report characterizes the pattern as a 'coercive environment' contributing to forcible transfer of Palestinian communities under international humanitarian law. Director Allegra Pacheco stated settlers are 'attacking communities in homes, domestic spaces — using sexualized violence as well.' The Consortium cited a 2025 Yesh Din study finding 93.6% of settler violence investigations ended without indictment, and argued that structural impunity enables ongoing violence. The report is part of growing international legal documentation of settler conduct in the West Bank that may support future ICC or ICJ proceedings.

media.caption: West Bank Protection Consortium report documents settler sexual violence against Palestinians since October 2023, amid structural impunity
